description: capital and largest city of Poland
description_wiki: Warsaw, officially the Capital City of Warsaw, is the capital and largest city of Poland.

description: violent attack on an ethnic or religious group, either approved or conducted by the local authorities
description_wiki: A pogrom is a violent riot incited with the aim of massacring or expelling an ethnic or religious group, usually applied to attacks on Jews.

description: Jewish relief organization based in New York City
description_wiki: American Jewish Joint Distribution Committee, also known as Joint or JDC, is a Jewish relief organization based in New York City.

description: island in New York Harbor in the United States of America
description_wiki: Ellis Island is an island in New York Harbor, within the U.S. states of New Jersey and New York.

description: seat of Cumberland County, and largest city in State of Maine, United States
description_wiki: Portland is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Maine.

aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.

description: capital city of Georgia, United States
description_wiki: Atlanta is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Georgia.
The Great Depression was a severe global economic downturn from 1929 to 1939.

aliases: The City College of New York
City College of the City University of New York
Free Academy of the City of New York
College of the City of New York
description: senior college of the City University of New York
description_wiki: The City College of the City University of New York (also known as the City College of New York, or simply City College or CCNY) is a public research university within the City University of New York (CUNY) system in New York City.

description: aerial warfare branch of the United States Army from 1941 to 1947
description_wiki: The United States Army Air Forces (USAAF or AAF) was the major land-based aerial warfare service component of the United States Army and de facto aerial warfare service branch of the United States during and immediately after World War II (1941–1947).
World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: United States Army base in New Jersey
description_wiki: Fort Dix, the common name for the Army Support Activity (ASA) located at Joint Base McGuire–Dix–Lakehurst, is a United States Army post.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: New Jersey is a state located in both the Mid-Atlantic and Northeastern regions of the United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Ohio (  oh-HY-oh) is a state in the Midwestern region of the United States.

description: public research university in Columbus, Ohio, United States
description_wiki: The Ohio State University (Ohio State, tOSU, or OSU) is a public research university in Columbus, Ohio, United States.

description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

aliases: The town of Our Lady the Queen of the Angels of the Little Portion
description: seat of Los Angeles County, and largest city in California, United States
description_wiki: Los Angeles (often referred to by its initials, LA) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of California, and the commercial, financial, and cultural center of Southern California.
